key_insight: MA-ProofBench introduces the first formal theorem-proving benchmark focused on mathematical analysis, revealing that current LLMs struggle sharply with deep formal reasoning.
engineering_relevance: For theorem-proving systems, this benchmark provides a harder, more realistic testbed for measuring progress in advanced formal mathematics beyond algebra and elementary number theory.
method: 200 formalized theorems across 6 core topics and 27 subcategories
Two-tier design: Level I undergraduate and Level II Ph.D. qualifying difficulty
Human-led, LLM-assisted formalization with independent expert review
evidence_signal: On the benchmark, even the best model (GPT-5.5) reaches only 16% Pass@8 on Level I and 5% on Level II, with most models near 0% on Level II.
watch_item: Performance may be constrained by Mathlib hallucinations and incomplete proofs, so results should be interpreted as much about formalization reliability as raw reasoning ability.
paper_title: MA-ProofBench: A Two-Tiered Evaluation of LLMs for Theorem Proving in Mathematical Analysis
